title: Grounding World Simulation Models in a Real-World Metropolis
task: City-scale video generation grounded in real urban environments
inputs: Street-view images, camera trajectories, text prompts
outputs: Long-horizon urban video with spatial fidelity and temporal consistency
key_results: Generates spatially faithful videos over hundreds of meters; outperforms existing world models across Seoul, Busan, Ann Arbor
comments: Addressing real-world grounding through retrieval-augmented conditioning is practical but requires substantial synthetic data generation to handle sparsity.

title: AI Can Learn Scientific Taste
task: Learning scientific taste for high-impact research idea generation
model: (preference learning + policy optimization) Scientific Judge + Scientific Thinker
inputs: Pairs of high/low-citation papers; research idea proposals
outputs: Research idea judgements and novel research proposals
key_results: Scientific Judge outperforms GPT-5.2 and Gemini 3 Pro; generalizes to future years and unseen fields; Scientific Thinker generates higher-impact ideas than baselines
comments: Clever use of citation as a proxy for impact, but citation metrics are noisy and subject to gaming; preference learning on 700K pairs is resource-intensive.

title: OpenSeeker: Democratizing Frontier Search Agents by Fully Open-Sourcing Training Data
task: Open-source frontier-level web search agent training
inputs: Synthetic QA tasks with web graph traversal; trajectories from teacher LLMs
outputs: Search actions and answers with reasoning chains
key_results: 11.7k synthesized samples achieve SOTA on BrowseComp (29.5%), BrowseComp-ZH (48.4%); outperforms Tongyi DeepResearch; far exceeds DeepDive baseline
comments: Impressive data efficiency through clever synthetic data generation, but evaluation is limited to search-specific benchmarks; generalization to other agent tasks unclear.

title: Safe and Scalable Web Agent Learning via Recreated Websites
task: Safe scalable web agent training via synthetic environments
inputs: Real websites; agent trajectories and environment specifications
outputs: Executable synthetic environments; verifiable task rewards
key_results: Agents trained with VeriEnv generalize to unseen websites; achieve site-specific mastery through self-evolution; benefit from scaling training environments
comments: Decoupling environment from real-world interaction is pragmatic, but synthetic website fidelity and coverage of edge cases remain critical open questions.

title: ViFeEdit: A Video-Free Tuner of Your Video Diffusion Transformer
task: Controllable video editing without video training data
inputs: 2D image data; control conditions; text guidance
outputs: Edited videos with temporal consistency and spatial control
key_results: Achieves versatile video generation and editing trained solely on 2D images; maintains temporal consistency with minimal additional parameters
comments: Data efficiency via cross-modal transfer is valuable, but limited quantitative comparisons with existing video editing methods in abstract.

title: WebVR: Benchmarking Multimodal LLMs for WebPage Recreation from Videos via Human-Aligned Visual Rubrics
task: Evaluating MLLMs on video-conditioned webpage recreation
model: (multimodal) 19 evaluated models including GPT-4V, Gemini variants
key_results: 175 webpages across diverse categories; 96% human-rubric agreement on automatic evaluation; substantial gaps in fine-grained style and motion quality
comments: Well-designed benchmark with controlled synthesis pipeline avoids data leakage, but video-to-code generation remains far from practical accuracy.

title: Panoramic Affordance Prediction
model: (training-free coarse-to-fine pipeline) PAP with recursive visual routing
inputs: Ultra-high-resolution panoramic images (12k resolution); natural language queries
key_results: PAP-12K benchmark with 12k QA pairs and ultra-high-res images; existing methods fail on panoramic data; PAP significantly outperforms baselines
comments: Novel task with appropriate dataset, but practical deployment on robotics with panoramic cameras remains to be demonstrated.

title: Attention Residuals
task: Efficient residual connection design for large LLMs
model: (transformer architecture) Attention Residuals (AttnRes) and Block AttnRes
key_results: Consistent improvement across model sizes (8B-48B); 6x parameter efficiency; 281x lower prefill latency; 136x higher decoding throughput vs. baseline
comments: Addressing hidden-state dilution with learned attention is elegant, but integration with Kimi Linear's sparse activation makes isolated impact unclear.

title: FlashMotion: Few-Step Controllable Video Generation with Trajectory Guidance
task: Few-step trajectory-controllable video generation with distillation
model: (diffusion transformer + adapter) FlashMotion with hybrid training
inputs: Trajectory specifications; text prompts; video frames
outputs: High-quality trajectory-accurate videos in few denoising steps
key_results: FlashBench benchmark for long-sequence trajectories; surpasses existing distillation methods; maintains trajectory consistency in few-step generation
comments: Clever hybrid training strategy combining diffusion and adversarial objectives, but ablation on trajectory accuracy preservation details is limited.

title: MMOU: A Massive Multi-Task Omni Understanding and Reasoning Benchmark for Long and Complex Real-World Videos
task: Evaluating MLLMs on omni-modal long-form video understanding
model: (multimodal) 20+ models including GPT-4o, Claude, Gemini, and open-source variants
inputs: Long videos (varying length) with rich audio-visual-textual content
outputs: Answers to complex reasoning questions requiring multi-modal integration
key_results: MMOU: 15k questions, 9038 videos; best closed-source 64.2% accuracy; best open-source 46.8%; systematic failure modes in long-form understanding identified
comments: Comprehensive benchmark exposing real limitations, but 64% SOTA accuracy suggests task may be underspecified or require human annotation calibration.

title: HSImul3R: Physics-in-the-Loop Reconstruction of Simulation-Ready Human-Scene Interactions
task: Physics-aware 3D reconstruction of human-scene interactions for simulation
model: (bi-directional optimization pipeline) HSImul3R with RL and simulation feedback
inputs: Sparse-view images or monocular videos of human-scene interactions
outputs: Simulation-ready 3D reconstructions of humans and scenes
key_results: First stable HSI reconstructions deployable to real humanoid robots; HSIBench benchmark with diverse scenarios; physics-grounded refinement eliminates perception-simulation gap
comments: Physics-in-the-loop approach is forward-thinking for embodied AI, but robot deployment validation limited to claims in abstract.

title: MoKus: Leveraging Cross-Modal Knowledge Transfer for Knowledge-Aware Concept Customization
task: Knowledge-aware visual concept customization via cross-modal transfer
model: (text-to-image diffusion) MoKus with anchor representation and knowledge queries
inputs: Target visual concepts; textual knowledge descriptions
outputs: Customized generated images with encoded textual knowledge
key_results: KnowCusBench benchmark introduced; outperforms SOTA on concept customization; enables virtual concept creation and concept erasure; improves world knowledge benchmarks
comments: Cross-modal knowledge transfer insight is interesting, but quantitative improvements over baselines not provided in abstract.

title: FineRMoE: Dimension Expansion for Finer-Grained Expert with Its Upcycling Approach
task: Fine-grained expert design with dimension expansion in MoE
model: (mixture-of-experts) FineRMoE with bi-level routing and upcycling
key_results: 6x parameter efficiency; 281x lower prefill latency; 136x higher decoding throughput; consistent improvements across 10 benchmarks; successful upcycling from existing models
comments: Extending fine-graining to output dimension is novel, but routing complexity and memory overhead of bi-level design may limit adoption.

title: EvoClaw: Evaluating AI Agents on Continuous Software Evolution
task: Evaluating AI agents on continuous software evolution
model: (code generation agents) 12 frontier models across 4 frameworks
inputs: Software repositories with commit history; milestone specifications
outputs: Code changes maintaining system integrity over time
key_results: Performance drops from >80% on isolated tasks to ≤38% on continuous evolution; DeepCommit reconstructs semantic milestone DAGs; error accumulation severely degrades long-term performance
comments: Critical finding exposing limitations of current agents in temporal/maintenance scenarios, but benchmark construction from commits introduces potential data quality issues.

title: Code-A1: Adversarial Evolving of Code LLM and Test LLM via Reinforcement Learning
task: Adversarial co-evolution of code and test generation LLMs
model: (reinforcement learning framework) Code-A1 with Code LLM vs. Test LLM
inputs: Problem specifications; candidate code; generated test suites
outputs: High-quality code and adversarial test cases
key_results: Matches or exceeds performance of models trained on human-annotated tests (Qwen2.5-Coder); significantly improves test generation; eliminates self-collusion risks via architectural separation
comments: Clever adversarial framing elegantly avoids self-collusion, but evaluation limited to code generation; impact on downstream applications unclear.

title: Mind the Shift: Decoding Monetary Policy Stance from FOMC Statements with Large Language Models
task: Detecting monetary policy stance shifts in FOMC statements
outputs: Continuous hawkish-dovish stance scores; inter-meeting shifts
key_results: 71.1% accuracy on sentence-level classification; strong correlation with inflation; significant association with Treasury yields; outperforms supervised and LLM-as-judge baselines
comments: Self-supervised temporal consistency framing is elegant for domain where manual labels are contentious, but economic validation on Treasury yields is post-hoc.
